open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — tracked, no server impact (a user can only exhaust their own browser tab). The viewer.js loadDocument() LOAD boundary still has no numPages>1000 / cumulative-byte cap and no processing timeout: after getDocument(...).promise it calls renderAll() (TASK-316 supersede-race guard confirmed present at viewer.js:118-129, so footprint is now linear, one canvas per page, not multiplied). Per-page / whole-doc consumers all sit downstream of this one uncapped boundary: (1) viewer.js renderAll() one canvas/page; (2) thumbnails.js a 2nd canvas/page (mitigated by 120px width, dpr cap 2, sequential render, renderToken guard); (3) merge.js mergeAndDownload() loads base.getData()+each queued file at once with no cumulative byte/page cap; (4) NEW this tick: pages.js (TASK-328) downloadRotated() and page-numbers.js (TASK-329) applyPageNumbers() both currentDoc.getData()→pdf-lib.load()→save() over ALL pages of the already-open doc — additional per-page consumers, NOT new load boundaries (they operate on the already-validated open document). Single right fix = developer task: a page-count/byte cap at the viewer.js LOAD boundary protects viewer + thumbnails + merge + pages + page-numbers + every future per-page consumer at once. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

audit_notes.innerHTML_in_js: only empty-string clears (container/list/layer .innerHTML='') or comments — no user/PDF content sink anywhere in the 22-file tree
audit_notes.inline_scripts_index_html: none — no <script>inline</script>, no on*= handlers, no javascript: URIs; only 2 same-origin script tags (lib/pdf-lib.min.js defer, js/app.js module) — CSP-safe
audit_notes.external_fetch_or_xhr: none (100% client-side, PDFs never leave the browser)
audit_notes.pages_js: TASK-328 rotate+download. baseName() strips .pdf, replaces [^a-zA-Z0-9._-] with _, slice(0,180), defaults 'document'; filename rotated-<base>.pdf — path-traversal + XSS safe. downloadBytes: Blob(application/pdf)+createObjectURL+a.download+rel=noopener, anchor removed, URL revoked after 1s. All status via textContent. Operates on already-open doc via getData(); no new load boundary.
audit_notes.page_numbers_js: TASK-329 stamp page numbers+download. buildFileName() same sanitizer -> <base>_numbered.pdf. Label content is numeric only (start clamped 0..1000000 + index) and goes to pdf-lib drawText, never the DOM. Same safe download pattern. status via textContent. Operates on already-open doc via getData(); no new load boundary.
audit_notes.merge_js_filename: buildFileName() strips to [a-zA-Z0-9._-] + '_merged.pdf'; displayed filename via textContent only — safe (unchanged this run)
audit_notes.secrets_scan_hit: only .gitignore '*.secret' pattern, changelog feature mentions, and tasks-archive 'secrets-audit' feature references — all false positives, no real secret
